New card — Replaced And Make Enough for Friends

Make Enough for Friends felt a bit like a rare, and given we aren't happy with the rare cycle at the moment, I figured reworking it at a higher rarity and making space for a "less narrow" (not that Make Enough had a particuarly bad or narrow floor, to be fair) uncommon recipe was a good path for both the uncommon and rare blue recipes.

If one or more counters would be put on an artifact, creature, or land you control, twice that many of each of those kinds of counters are put on that permanent instead.
cards.notes: Reworked from Doubling Season — People didn't like Doubling's interaction with walkers

cards.rules_text:
This creature enters with four +1/+1 counters on it.
If damage would be dealt to this creature while it has a +1/+1 counter on it, prevent that damage, remove a +1/+1 counter from this creature, and create a Food token. [i](It's an artifact with “{2}, {T}, Sacrifice this token: You gain 3 life.”)[/i]
cards.notes:
New card — Replaced Nocinomrahnap

Nocinomrahnap was used on Sid, making it redundant.

cards.rules_text:
{T}: Add {C}.
{X}{X}, {T}: Put X meal counters on this land.
Whenever you cast a creature spell, you may remove a meal counter from this land. If you do, that spell gains nourish 1. [i](When that creature enters, put a +1/+1 counter on it or create a colorless Food artifact token with “{2}, {T}, Sacrifice this artifact: You gain 3 life.”)[/i]
cards.notes:
New card — Replaced Contested Cliffs

Cliffs feels like it should be a commander card rather than a main set card. It also "arbitrarily" gave RG an extra "multicolored" card in a set where the multicolor pairs are very balanced.

cards.rules_text:
Flying
When this creature leaves the battlefield, create a Food token. [i](It's an artifact with “{2}, {T}, Sacrifice this token: You gain 3 life.”)[/i]
{2}{W}, Pay 3 life: Return this creature from your graveyard to the battlefield tapped.
cards.notes:
Changed art
1RW —> 1WW
Want to gauge the reactions to this in mono-W. There's nothing here that white can't do, really, and I think a one-off white phoenix isn't really offensive.

cards.rules_text:
Devour artifact and/or creature 2 [i](As this enters, you may sacrifice any number of artifacts and/or creatures. This creature enters with twice that many +1/+1 counters on it.)[/i]
When Oakenmaul enters, choose one for each Beast it devoured–
• Put two +1/+1 counters on Oakenmaul.
• You gain 3 life.
• Draw a card.
cards.notes:
New card — Replaced Mana Garden

Garden feels like it wants to be in a commander precon, and I want at least one mythic to care about Beasts.

cards.rules_text: At the beginning of your upkeep, you lose 3 life and create a Food token. [i](It's an artifact with “{2}, {T}, Sacrifice this artifact: You gain 3 life.”)[/i]
cards.flavor_text: [i]“I've spent my whole life being a pest. To be welcomed into a kitchen is an honor.”[/i]
cards.rules_text2:
{2}, {T}, Sacrifice Charcuterie Fantastique: You gain 3 life.
When you sacrifice Charcuterie Fantastique, you may exile it. If you do, at the beginning of the next end step, return to the battlefield target artifact or creature card in your graveyard with mana value less than or equal to the amount of life you've gained this turn or the amount of life you've lost this turn, whichever is greater. [i](Damage causes loss of life.)[/i]

+Legendary backside
Reworked backside — moved original effect to Signature Creations File

Functional reprint of Ancestor's Aid — Replaced Freneticism

I spent a good amount of time trying to figure out a version of Freneticism that felt strong, but not too strong, and also not too weak, but it seems like such a fine balance to strike that it's hard to make it work and be appealing on a common. We also have a lot of leaves the battlefield stuff amoung the creatures, and I don't think we need another such effect on a combat trick of all things.

> If a spell or ability requires multiple players to face a villainous choice, the result is a little different from other effects in Magic. In that case, the first player in turn order makes their choice and the action for that choice is performed before the next player makes their choice. Then each of the remaining players repeat this process in turn order.

Choose one –
• Return target creature you control and all Auras you control attached to it to their owner's hand.
• Destroy target creature and you lose life equal to its toughness.
• Return target creature card with mana value 1 or less from your graveyard to the battlefield.

Choose one –

• Destroy target enchantment.

cards.flavor_text: [i]Murky Otarian tides hide the forgotten treacheries of the Phyrexian war.[/i]
[i]Gnolls make offerings to sinister forces in the shadow-drenched chasms.[/i]
[i]From Otaria, the sun above meets the sea below at a glittering horizon.[/i]
[i]Silence, the poetry of a whispering wind, the centurial fall of tar on pale sand.[/i]
[i]Stability and volatility churn together, creating beauty and danger both.[/i]
[i]Still to the ignorant eye, yet bursting with life. A million actions and reactions, unstoppable as the dawn.[/i]
[i]Life and death mixing together, both ends of the same cycle giving rise to something else.[/i]
[i]Majesty rising above the arid sweep of land, a bulwark horizon against the mundane, a divide cleaving inspiration from stagnation.[/i]
